data.text: I’m adding a shared test context now so the package tests can be migrated mechanically without spraying `context.Background()` through every file. That keeps the review surface small and makes the later compile errors easier to reason about.

I’m doing the repetitive test-side call-site rewrite in one mechanical pass now. This is the part where a script is the least risky option: it touches only the Store receivers that need the new ctx argument and leaves non-Store calls alone.

input.command: /usr/bin/zsh -lc "for f in internal/bead/*_test.go; do perl -0pi -e 's/\\b((?:s|store|src|dst|s2|beadStore|pw\\.store|s\\.store))\\.(Init|ReadAll|Get|Create|Apply)\\(/"'$1.$2(testCtx, /g'"' \""'$f"; done'

data.text: The mechanical rewrite did its job, but the zero-argument methods picked up a dangling comma. I’m normalizing those now so the test files read cleanly as `Init(testCtx)` and `ReadAll(testCtx)`.

input.command: /usr/bin/zsh -lc "for f in internal/bead/*_test.go; do perl -0pi -e 's/\\.Init\\(testCtx, \\)/.Init(testCtx)/g; s/\\.ReadAll\\(testCtx, \\)/.ReadAll(testCtx)/g' \""'$f"; done'

data.text: There are a couple of holdouts in the `internal/bead/axon` test subpackage and one archive test using a Store named `archive`. I’m patching those explicit leftovers rather than broadening the regex further.

data.output:
package bead

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// archiveTestStore returns a fresh active store rooted at a temp .ddx dir.
func archiveTestStore(t *testing.T) (*Store, string) {
	t.Helper()
	dir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755))
	s := NewStore(dir)
	require.NoError(t, s.Init(testCtx))
	return s, dir
}

// closedBeadAt builds a closed bead whose UpdatedAt is the given time.
func closedBeadAt(id, title string, updated time.Time) Bead {
	return Bead{
		ID:        id,
		Title:     title,
		Status:    StatusClosed,
		Priority:  2,
		IssueType: DefaultType,
		CreatedAt: updated.Add(-time.Hour),
		UpdatedAt: updated,
	}
}

func TestArchiveMovesEligibleClosedBeads(t *testing.T) {
	s, dir := archiveTestStore(t)
	old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour)
	beads := []Bead{
		closedBeadAt("ddx-old1", "old closed 1", old),
		closedBeadAt("ddx-old2", "old closed 2", old),
		{
			ID:        "ddx-fresh",
			Title:     "fresh closed",
			Status:    StatusClosed,
			Priority:  2,
			IssueType: DefaultType,
			CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC().Add(-time.Hour),
			UpdatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
		},
		{
			ID:        "ddx-open",
			Title:     "still open",
			Status:    StatusOpen,
			Priority:  2,
			IssueType: DefaultType,
			CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC().Add(-time.Hour),
			UpdatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
		},
	}
	require.NoError(t, s.WriteAll(beads))

	policy := DefaultArchivePolicy()
	policy.MinActiveCount = 0
	moved, err := s.Archive(policy)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.ElementsMatch(t, []string{"ddx-old1", "ddx-old2"}, moved)

	// AC1: archive file is written.
	archivePath := filepath.Join(dir, BeadsArchiveCollection+".jsonl")
	info, err := os.Stat(archivePath)
	require.NoError(t, err, "beads-archive.jsonl must exist")
	assert.Greater(t, info.Size(), int64(0))

	// Active retains only the fresh-closed and open beads.
	remaining, err := s.ReadAll(testCtx)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	ids := make([]string, 0, len(remaining))
	for _, b := range remaining {
		ids = append(ids, b.ID)
	}
	assert.ElementsMatch(t, []string{"ddx-fresh", "ddx-open"}, ids)

	// Archive contains the moved beads with archived_at set.
	archive := s.archivePartner()
	archived, err := archive.ReadAll()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Len(t, archived, 2)
	for _, b := range archived {
		stamp, ok := b.Extra["archived_at"].(string)
		assert.True(t, ok, "archived bead %s should carry archived_at", b.ID)
		assert.NotEmpty(t, stamp)
	}
}

func TestArchiveRespectsMinActiveCount(t *testing.T) {
	s, dir := archiveTestStore(t)
	old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour)
	require.NoError(t, s.WriteAll([]Bead{
		closedBeadAt("ddx-old1", "old", old),
		closedBeadAt("ddx-old2", "old2", old),
	}))

	policy := DefaultArchivePolicy()
	policy.MinActiveCount = 100
	moved, err := s.Archive(policy)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, moved, "trigger must not fire below MinActiveCount")

	_, statErr := os.Stat(filepath.Join(dir, BeadsArchiveCollection+".jsonl"))
	if statErr == nil {
		// File may exist from Init() but must be empty.
		archive := s.archivePartner()
		archived, _ := archive.ReadAll()
		assert.Empty(t, archived)
	}
}

func TestArchivePreservesReferencedClosedBeads(t *testing.T) {
	s, _ := archiveTestStore(t)
	old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour)
	beads := []Bead{
		closedBeadAt("ddx-dep", "closed dep", old),
		{
			ID:        "ddx-open",
			Title:     "open with closed dep",
			Status:    StatusOpen,
			Priority:  2,
			IssueType: DefaultType,
			CreatedAt: old,
			UpdatedAt: old,
			Dependencies: []Dependency{
				{IssueID: "ddx-open", DependsOnID: "ddx-dep", Type: "blocks"},
			},
		},
	}
	require.NoError(t, s.WriteAll(beads))

	policy := DefaultArchivePolicy()
	policy.MinActiveCount = 0
	moved, err := s.Archive(policy)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, moved, "closed dep referenced by an open bead must stay active")

	remaining, err := s.ReadAll(testCtx)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Len(t, remaining, 2)
}

func TestArchiveGetWithArchiveFallsBack(t *testing.T) {
	s, _ := archiveTestStore(t)
	old := time.Now().UTC().Add(-60 * 24 * time.Hour)
	require.NoError(t, s.WriteAll([]Bead{
		closedBeadAt("ddx-archived", "to be archived", old),
		{
			ID:        "ddx-active",
			Title:     "still active",
			Status:    StatusOpen,
			Priority:  2,
			IssueType: DefaultType,
			CreatedAt: old,
			UpdatedAt: old,
		},
	}))

	policy := DefaultArchivePolicy()
	policy.MinActiveCount = 0
	moved, err := s.Archive(policy)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, []string{"ddx-archived"}, moved)

	// AC2: show resolves IDs from either collection.
	b, err := s.GetWithArchive("ddx-archived")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "to be archived", b.Title)

	b, err = s.GetWithArchive("ddx-active")
	require.NoError(t, err)

data.text: I caught one sloppy substitution in the axon smoke test: the store init happens before a request context exists. I’m fixing that to `context.Background()` and using `r.Context()` inside the handler where a request context actually exists.
The internal package is mostly migrated. I’m running a narrower search for any leftover zero-arg Store calls or syntax damage before I move on to the command/server surface.

data.text: The new compile errors show the package is still seeing the old `Store` method shapes somewhere, so I’m verifying the live source at the `Create` definition and the `Backend` assertion before I touch more tests.

The compile error on `chaos2_test.go` suggests that file may be exercising a different store-like helper, not `*Store` directly. I’m opening that test’s fixture type so I can tell whether it needs the new `Store` API or a separate helper migration.

I found the root of the compile churn: the chaos suite is still typed against the legacy `Backend` interface, which is why the ctx-first `Create`/`ReadAll` calls won’t land cleanly. I’m checking how many test helpers actually depend on that interface so I can narrow the migration to concrete `*Store` in the tests instead of expanding the production interface.

The cleanest fix is to make the chaos fixtures concrete `*Store` instead of the legacy `Backend` interface. That preserves the new ctx-first `Create`/`ReadAll` calls without forcing the rest of the legacy API to change.

I’ve separated the chaos suite from the legacy `Backend` type, so the `Store` no longer needs to pretend it implements that old interface. I’m dropping that assertion now and will keep the new interface coverage in the dedicated test file.

func TestClaimLeaseCanonicalizesProjectRootAliases(t *testing.T) {
	withHeartbeat(t, 5*time.Millisecond, 50*time.Millisecond)

	root := t.TempDir()
	realRoot := filepath.Join(root, "repo")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(realRoot, ".ddx"), 0o755))
	aliasRoot := filepath.Join(root, "repo-alias")
	require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(realRoot, aliasRoot))

	sReal := NewStore(filepath.Join(realRoot, ".ddx"))
	sAlias := NewStore(filepath.Join(aliasRoot, ".ddx"))
	b := &Bead{ID: "ddx-hb-alias", Title: "Live claim through alias"}
	require.NoError(t, sReal.Create(b))
	require.NoError(t, sReal.Claim(b.ID, "worker-a"))

	deadline := time.Now().Add(150 * time.Millisecond)
	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
		require.NoError(t, sReal.TouchClaimHeartbeat(b.ID))
		time.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)
	}

	err := sAlias.Claim(b.ID, "worker-b")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "cannot claim")

	ready, err := sAlias.ReadyExecution()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Empty(t, ready)

	got, err := sReal.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "worker-a", got.Owner)
}

data.text: I’m starting with the agent package because the first module run showed it as the immediate blocker. Where there’s a real request context available, I’ll thread it through; otherwise I’ll use a background context to keep the migration bounded.

I’m collecting the receiver names behind the remaining Store calls before I bulk-edit anything. That lets me patch the obvious store-like names in one pass without trampling unrelated `Get`/`ReadAll` calls on non-bead types.

input.command: /usr/bin/zsh -lc "rg -o \"\\b[a-zA-Z0-9_.]+\\.(Init|ReadAll|Get|Create)\\(\" cmd internal/agent internal/agentmetrics internal/processmetrics internal/server | sed 's/\\.(Init\\|ReadAll\\|Get\\|Create)(//' | sort -u | sed -n '1,260p'"

data.text: The next layer of breakage is coming from local agent interfaces that still describe the old no-context bead API. I’m checking those definitions now because updating the interface contract there may be cheaper than patching every hook call site individually.

input.command: /usr/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"type BeadReader interface|type ExecuteBeadLoopStore interface|type BeadWriter interface|type .*Store interface\" internal/agent internal/exec internal/server | sed -n '1,220p'"

/usr/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' internal/agent/interfaces.go 2>/dev/null || true && printf '\\n---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go && printf '\\n---\\n' && sed -n '1,220p' internal/agent/lint_hook.go"

data.text: I’m stopping the implementation pass here rather than pushing a half-green repo. The remaining failures are the broader ctx-first migration in `cmd`, `internal/agent`, `internal/exec`, and `internal/server`, so I’m recording that explicitly as a decomposition request.

Stopped before commit.

- `go test ./internal/bead -count=1` passes.
- `go test ./... -count=1` still fails because the ctx-first `Store` API change ripples into `cmd`, `internal/agent`, `internal/exec`, `internal/processmetrics`, and `internal/server`.
- I recorded the blocker and decomposition request in [no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-215b08a2-20260512T202606-17af9511/.ddx/executions/20260512T202606-17af9511/no_changes_rationale.txt).
